nodes.statement: sigma(A_{N,c}) approximates sigma(K_c) in the bulk (Theorem A of Paper XXI)
D_Edge != 0: at least one positive eigenvalue (Theorem B of Paper XXI)
Gram coercivity implies spectral gap >= C c^{-1/2} (Paper 13b Theorem A)
K_c(x,y) = sin(c(x-y)) / (pi(x-y)): sinc kernel (Slepian 1978)
Widom edge phase phi_c and O(c) growth (Widom 1964)
Deift-Zhou steepest descent framework (Deift 1999)
Fredholm determinant continuity in S1-norm (standard)
Operator setup: D_Edge as mismatch form, Bulk vs Edge regimes
Exact IIKS identity: K_c(x,y) = F_c(x)^T J G_c(y) / (x-y)
nodes.source: Paper XXII, Section 2, exact algebraic identity from BB4
nodes.notes: This is a coordinate system, not an argument. The cubic Airy term does not arise from the Fourier representation; it requires integration of the Widom edge phase via IIKS RHP structure.

nodes.statement: The cubic Airy term 1/3 u^3 does not arise from the Fourier representation; it requires integration of the Widom edge phase via IIKS RHP structure
Edge stability decomposes as: (nonlinear phase consistency) + (linear kernel transfer)
Assumption A: exists g_c analytic satisfying nonlinear matching 2 Re(g_c^+) = Re(phi_c) with g_c(z) = log z + O(z^{-1})
nodes.notes: Nonlinear: phi_c = phi_c[g_c] (implicit coupling). No RMT candidate satisfies (A) for large c.

nodes.statement: Nonlinearity of Assumption A: phi_c = phi_c[g_c] (implicit dependence through spectral density near band edge); no RMT-type candidate satisfies (A) for large c
Assumption B: given Assumption A, steepest-descent geometry holds (sign condition, Lipschitz contours, no additional saddles)
nodes.notes: Standard Deift-Zhou once g_c is known from P5. P7 cannot be formulated without P5.

nodes.statement: Conjecture 3.3: Y_c -> Y_Airy uniformly on compacta; implies ||K_c^tilde - K^Airy||_{S1} = O(c^{-1/3})
Reduction: uniform edge-frame stability => lambda_min(G_{N,c}) >= alpha(c) ~ c^{-1/2} (Proposition 4.1)
nodes.notes: This is the new content of XXII. BB3 is then applied as black-box functor. Landau-Widom scale.

nodes.statement: Reduction functor via BB3: lambda_min(G_{N,c}) >= c^{-1/2} => gap lower bound >= C c^{-1/2} (Theorem 4.2, imported from Paper 13b)
nodes.source: Paper 13b Theorem A, applied as Theorem 4.2 in Paper XXII
nodes.notes: R1 is not re-proved. New content of XXII is P9 only.

nodes.statement: Conditional Airy-scale upgrade: under Conjecture 3.3, gap upgrades to c^{-1/3} (Corollary 4.1)
D^Airy = Pi_Airy (A^arith - K^Airy) Pi_Airy: Rand-Weyl operator definition (Proposition 5.1)
Conjecture 5.2 (main gap conjecture): inf sigma(D^Airy) >= c2^Airy > 0
Main Theorem (Theorem 5.3, conditional on P12): inf_{f in Edge, ||f||=1} <D_Edge f, f> >= c2^Airy / 2 > 0
nodes.notes: Terminal node. Conditional on P12 (O5) and implicitly on O4 via P9 -> R1.

nodes.statement: Prove Assumption A: exists g_c satisfying the nonlinear matching condition
nodes.mechanism: Fixed-point operator T_c(g) = H_{(-1,1)}[1/2 Re(phi_c[g])] + log(cdot); prove T_c contractive on a Banach space of analytic functions via Muskhelishvili theory.

nodes.statement: Prove Assumption B given A: steepest-descent geometry holds
nodes.mechanism: Standard Deift-Zhou: sign check on Re(2g_c - phi_c) along contour, Lipschitz parameterisation. No new method required once g_c is known from O1.

nodes.statement: Uniform BW-Doubling: inf_{n in [(1-delta)N, N)} E_out(|psi_n|^2) >= c3 > 0 (alternate route to P9)
nodes.mechanism: Extend Paper III pointwise argument to a uniform Schur-test on the edge block; alternatively transfer via Kadec-1/4 for prime frequencies in edge band.

nodes.statement: Uniform quasi-isometry: A||f||^2 <= ||Ev f||^2 <= B||f||^2 for all f in Edge, with A > 0 uniform in N
nodes.mechanism: Frame stability via prime-frequency sampling in edge band. Three routes: (A) Uniform BW-Doubling, (B) Jaffard Gram matrix inversion, (C) Beurling-Kadec for edge-restricted PW. See O4_frame_stability_problem.tex.

nodes.statement: inf sigma(D^Airy) >= c2^Airy > 0
nodes.mechanism: Three routes: (A) Airy zero oscillation bounds, (B) Tracy-Widom constant comparison, (C) Fredholm resolvent via IIKS. See O5_airy_gap_problem.tex.

nodes.statement: Under RH: characterise RH-collapse of the coercivity gap
nodes.mechanism: sigma(D^Airy) controlled by zero distribution of zeta(s); coercivity failure iff accumulation of zeros off critical line.
